components.description: Responsible for configuration relating to DER capabilities that the EVSE of the Charging Station can emulate by using ISO 15118-20 ChargeLoop messages to control the inverter in the EV. The component is located at the EVSE level, since it represents the DER capabilities of the EVSE.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the reporting of clock-aligned meter data.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the use of a local cache for authorization for Charging Station use.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the use of authorization for Charging Station use.
Responsible for configuration relating to Battery swapping.
A CHAdeMO Controller component communicates with an EV using the wired CANbus protocol to exchange information and control charging using the CHAdeMO protocol
Provides a means to configure management of time tracking by Charging Station.
Responsible for configuration relating to custom vendor-specific implementations, like the DataTransfer message and CustomData extensions or CustomTriggers.

Responsible for configuration relating to DER capabilities of the DC inverter of the EVSE in the Charging Station. The component is located at the EVSE level, since it represents the DER capabilities, also referred to as nameplate information, of the EVSE.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the exchange and storage of Charging Station Device Model data.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the display of messages to Charging Station users.
Communicates with an EV to exchange information and control charging using the ISO 15118 protocol.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the use of Local Authorization Lists for Charging Station use.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the exchange of monitoring event data.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to payment terminals.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to information exchange between Charging Station and CSMS.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to reservations.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to the reporting of sampled meter data.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to security of communications between Charging Station and CSMS.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to smart charging.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to tariff and cost display.
Logical Component responsible for configuration relating to transaction characteristics and behaviour.
Responsible for configuration relating to V2X charging/discharging. This component exists on the EVSE tier hierarchy.
Responsible for configuration of a dynamic QR code for ad hoc payments.
Allows physical access of vehicles to a charging site to be controlled.
Provides a variable DC current source to force energy directly into an EV battery stack, under tight control of the EV's battery management system.
Allows a specific AC phase to be selected (typically at EVSE tier) for single phase vehicle charging in order to lower overall (e.g. site) phase imbalance.
A general purpose electro-mechanical output system, with optional completion tracking sensing. Each output should use a Variable instance key indicating the nature of the output.
Fans (or equivalent devices) used to provide cooling.
Fans (or equivalent devices) used to ensure that EVs that require ventilation during charging
BatteryCartridge represents the battery cartridge that is currently inserted into the EVSE of a battery swap station
Sensor (optical, ground loop, ultrasonic, etc.) to detect whether the associated parking/charging bay is physically vacant, or is occupied by a vehicle or other obstruction
Beacon Lighting to help EV drivers to locate nearby charging places, and/or to determine charging availability state, usually by color variation.
A sensor that detects when a charging cable (captive or removable) has been forcibly pulled from the Charging Station.
Reports when an access door/panel is open
The entire Charging Station as a logical entity
The Charging Status Indicator, provides visible feedback to the user about the connection and charging status of an EVSE/Connector. This is commonly in the form of multi-colored lighting.

ConnectedEV is a component that represents a connected vehicle for which data is received via an ISO 15118 or CHAdeMO interface. The generic information that is received, is represented as variables of ConnectedEV. Any protocol-specific information is represented in variables of the ISO15118Ctrlr or CHAdeMOCtrlr component.
A means to connect an EV to a Charging Station with either a socket, an attached cable & inline connector, or any wireless power transfer device.
A mechanism present in a connector holster to prevent the connector from being removed inappropriately: typically unlocks connector after authorization.
A mechanism to report when a tethered cable connector has been removed from its normal stowage position. May be used for detection of connectors left un-holstered, and possible penalty billing.
Locking mechanism to retain an inserted plug, both to prevent on-load disconnection, and to prevent theft of charging cables
External protective mechanism (e.g. an external shutter or a connector holster lock mechanism) to prevent contact with conductors that may become 'live' under other failure modes
An embedded logic controller
Energy, Power, Electricity meter, used to measure energy, current, voltages etc.
Control Pilot PWM Controller: provides and senses the IEC 61851-1 / SAE J1772 low voltage DC and PWM signalling between an EVSE and EV over a control pilot line.
Provides a communications link from a Charging Station to a CSMS. It may use fixed infrastructure, mobile telephony data services, WiFi, or other connectivity channels.
Provides information and feedback to the user.
Defines the Distribution Panel, with it's fuses and connections to both Charging Stations and other Distribution Panel's.

Represents an incoming electrical connection to a Charging Station, that may be a grid/distribution network connection, of a connection to local power generation and/or storage. Each electrical feed can record the electrical and other characteristics of that feed, including power rating, fusing, upstream metering, etc. When a Charging Station has more than one electrical feed, it must represent which feed supplies each EVSE, and which feed supplies the house load of the Charging Station itself. Simple Charging Stations with only a single electrical feed may omit all electrical feed information, in which case it is inferred that all power is supplied from a single feed, and what would otherwise be ElectricalFeed data (Variables) may be reported as being associated with the ChargingStation component.
Represents the low voltage power supply (typically 12V DC and often other ELV voltages) that provides operating power for controllers, relays, and other electrical components.
An 'Emergency Stop' button that should be pressed by the user or other nearby persons if serious faulty behavior is observed (e.g. smoke/flames from EV or Charging Station).
Provides reporting/control of general illumination lighting in use at Charging Station.
A locking mechanism on the EV side as a safety measure to prevent it being disconnected while high currents are flowing.
The entire chain of components responsible for transporting energy from the incoming supply to the electric vehicle (or vice versa)
Reports ambient air temperature
Provides energy transfer readings that are the basis for billing.
A sensor reporting whether the Charging Station is experiencing water ingress/pooling.
An Isolation Tester as part of their own self-test mechanisms, to confirm the isolation of floating circuitry when no Evs are connected
Heater to ensure reliable operation in cold environments
Reports relative air humidity
Reports ambient light levels.
A liquid based cooling system, typically used to cool the connector cables of very high power Charging Stations.

Accepts local signal inputs controlling whether new Charging Sessions can start and/or whether ongoing sessions should continue. Typically connected to a site/building power supply, to automatically report unavailability when closed.
The entire Local Controller as a logical entity
Energy storage
The instances of component NetworkConfiguration represent network connection configurations.
Protects equipment by disconnecting the electrical supply when the current drawn (on any phase) exceeds the rated value to a substantial degree.
Recloser mechanism of an OverCurrentProtection to perform re-arm retries after a trip, or may be set for remotely controlled re-arming on command.
Switches on and off the power to the EV after all authorization and safety requirements have been met. May have secondary contacts to report closure state.

A Residual Current Device (US: ground fault breaker) protects human life and/or downstream equipment by quickly detecting abnormal current flows (usually indicative in earth faults) in the Charging Station, cable, or EV during charging.
A motorized recloser mechanism of an RCD that may be configured to perform re-arm retries after a trip, or may be set for remotely controlled re-arming on command.
Represents realtime clock hardware that can maintain accurate date & time information in a Charging Station, even in the case of simultaneous CSMS uncontactability and power outages or resets.
Measures impact forces/accelerations experienced, indicative of possible damage.
Electronic signage allowing a charging controller for a large charging facility to advertise counts of available spaces to passing traffic.
A general purpose electromechanical input device, with optional remote defaulting/resetting of values. Each input should use a Variable instance key indicating the nature of the input.

Temperature sensor at a point inside the Charging Station, multiple sensing points for a single sensing controller. Multiple sensing points for a single sensing controller may be reported using distinct Variable instance keys.
Measures Tilt angle from normal reference position (normally 90 degree vertical).
An authorization token reader (e.g. RFID)
Circuitry designed to trigger the disconnection of power to the structure by an upstream protection device after a severe problem has been detected

A logical input mechanism (e.g. set of buttons) that is part of a UI whose use may be communicated to the CSMS (in near real time). May support momentary inputs ('Operated') or modal state ('Active'). Multiple input sources should use explicit Variable instance keys (where the input function is key name).

Reports an identifier associated with a vehicle occupying a charging bay. The identifier may be a vehicle registration number via ANPR hardware, a VIN, or other local identifier of the vehicle based on medium range/active RFID, or any other relevant technology and result.

dm_components_vars.description: RMS AC Current (in amperes). For 3-phase circuits, each phase (and optional neutral) is represented by a Variable instance equal to a value of the PhaseEnumType (e.g. L1,N). Unkeyed values reported for a Component declared to be multi-phase are assumed to be an average of all per-phase readings and written values are common per-phase settings. Example(s): ChargingStation: Total AC current consumption (all EVSEs, ancillaries), EVSE: Total current consumed by EVSE: includes losses (AC->DC) and EVSE specific ancillaries (e.g. fans), ElectricalFeed: Inflow AC current on feed

Component is in its non-resting / active state: e.g: On, Engaged, Locked. Some Components may have secondary functions that have corresponding Active Variables with an explicit Variable instance., Note: Monitoring of changes in the Active state of any Component can be specified by setting Delta monitoring on the boolean value with a delta values of 1. Setting/clearing an Active Variable activates/stops the associated functionality, where remotely controllable. Only components that are Available and Enabled can be in the Active state.

RMS AC Voltage (in volts). For 3-phase circuits, each phase (and optional neutral) is represented by a Variable instance equal to a value of the PhaseEnumType (e.g. L1,N). Unkeyed values reported for a Component declared to be multi-phase are assumed to be an average ofall per-phase readings and written values are common per-phase settings. Example(s): ElectricalFeed: Input Voltage
Component can be reset.
Angle(s) relative to normal/design idle position. Multiple Variable instance values may be used to indicate angular position in multiple axes (e.g. Left-Right, Forward-Back).

Number of attempts (INCLUDING the original attempt) in the last successful or attempted, cycle of operation. Applies typically to self-monitoring motorized electro-mechanical equipment, etc. {Null}: Unknown, 0: Not Attempted/Not allowed, 1: Single attempt/No retries [allowed], 2-N: [up to] N tries [allowed]
The Component exists and is locally configured/wired for use, but might not be (remotely) Enabled.
Digital Certificate (in Base64 encoding)

Standard 24 bit hexadecimal RGB values. Reg Green Blue color intensity, expressed as standard 24 bit hexadecimal RGB values: 3  00-FF (0-255), in order RRGGBB). E.g. 000000: Black, FF0000: Red, 00FF00: Green, 0000FF: Blue, FFFF00:Yellow, FFFFFF: White, 008000: Medium intensity green.
Component operation cycle has completed. Used only in event notifications, where it is always true.
Time since logical connection established
General purpose integer count variable for Component state reporting
Percentage current imbalance in an AC three phase supply.
Text associated with a Component, e.g. a Display.
Point in time value, in [RFC3339] datetime format. Time zone optional.
DC Current (in amperes). May be an instantaneous measurement, or a period average, depending on context/equipment.
DC Voltage (volts). May be an instantaneous measurement, or a period average, depending on context/equipment.
Production series variants reflecting internal design changes or sub-component substitutions not affecting external functionality.

The Component is Enabled for operation. For Available components that cannot be selectively (remotely) enabled / disabled, this value is always true. Note: Available cannot be false of Enabled is true, so during inventory reporting, Enabled=1 also logically states Available=true
Energy quantity (in Wh) for reporting/configuring values related to stored energy (i.e. not transferred energy).
General purpose variable for reporting/managing numbers of entries in repetitive data structures. maxLimit characteristic reports maximum possible entries.

Component is operating in a fallback, or backup mode. In inventory reports, a Value of 1 for the maxLimit characteristic indicates that the component can enter a fallback state (i.e. a fallback mode is present).
Fan Speed (in RPM). A value of 0 represents stopped/stalled. An empty value indicates that fan speed cannot be read.
Version number of firmware.

Reports (impact) force/ acceleration values (estimates) in one or more directions, in units of Newtons or g. Multiple force readings in different (orthogonal) dimensions may be reported using Variable instance values, such as Down, Right, Forward.
List of message formats supported by this Charging Station. Possible values: ASCII, HTML, URI, UTF-8.
Frequency of AC power, signal, or component operation.
Current rating of a fuse/breaker. Variable instances keyed by phase identifier (L1/L2/L3/N).
Height above(+)/below(-) reference level (ground level unless context demands otherwise).
The relative humidity in %.

Specifies the width of a 'dead band' (as a percentage of the threshold) around the central value of a threshold setting (e.g. MinSet, MaxSet, monitor thresholds) to avoid repeated triggering when the measured quantity lies close to the threshold and is subject to small variations.
ICCID (Integrated Circuit Card IDentifier) of mobile data SIM card.

Impedance: Primary value is real (resistive only) impedance. Where a complex impedance is to be reported, the imaginary part (reactance) must be represented with a separate Variable instance value of 'reactance'. Reactance values are expressed at the (nominal) relevant operating frequency of the Component (e.g. 50/60Hz for mains electricity feed).
IMSI (International Mobile Subscriber Identity) number of mobile data SIM card
Minimum Interval (in seconds) between (attempted) operations.
General Purpose linear distance measure.
(Ambient) light level. The value is in Lux.
Component Manufacturer name
Specific stored message for display.
Minimum duration that a Charging Station or EVSE status is stable before StatusNotificationRequest is sent to the CSMS.
Operating mode string from among valid options (communicated by OptionList, etc. during capability/configuration  discovery).

Manufacturer's Model code/number of Component, including suffixes etc. to identify functional, regional or linguistic variation, but NOT engineering change level internal 				variation not affecting external behaviour, etc.
Current network address of a Component.
The Component operated in an instantaneous, transient, or immediately self-resetting pattern. Used only in event notifications, where it is always true.
Recurring operating times in iCalendar RRULE format.
Component is in Overload state.
Generic dimensionless value reporting/setting value.

The phase wiring of Component, relative to it's upstream feed Component/device. This variable describes the phase rotation of a Component relative to its parent Component, using a three letter string consisting of the letters: R, S, T and x. The letter 'R' can be identified as phase 1 (L1), 'S' as phase 2 (L2), 'T' as phase 3 (L3). The lower case 'x' is used to designate a phase that is not connected. An empty string means that phase rotation is not applicable or not known.
Elapsed time in seconds since last substantive energy transfer

Instantaneous (real) Power (measured/calculated, including power factor for AC). Where a component (e.g. AC to DC Power Converter) has multiple power measurements, the default (unkeyed) instance is input power.

Component itself has a 'Problem' condition that impacts in any significant way on its normal operation. By definition, 'Problem' state includes (logical OR) 'Fault' state. 'Problem' specifically INCLUDES inability to operate that is propagated (up/down/sideways) from any other associated/connected/containing/contained Component.

Applies to 'sensor' type Components that have an associated protection capability, whereby they can  disconnect power (e.g. using the main PowerContactor) if the sensed quantity is outside preset/configured limits. If Protecting is true, the Component is actively preventing/interrupting charging.
Serial number of Component.
(Radio/Wired/Optical) data signal strength, in ASU (typically 0-31 or 99 for unknown). Or dbmW (typically -140 to -50).
A state code or name identifier string, to allow the internal state of  components to be reported and/or  controlled

Energy Storage Device (e.g. battery) state of charge, expressed as a percentage of nominal design 0-100% operating range. Note: Values below or above 0-100% are possible and represent over discharged/charged states.
In bytes. Amount of storage occupied. Storage(maxLimit) specifies absolute limit Storage(MaxSet) restricts usage to specified Max, if supported.
Number of alternating current phases connected/available. 1 or 3 for AC, 0 means DC (no alternating phases). Null value indicates that the number of phases (e.g. in use) is unknown.
If Suspending is true, the Component can is currently suspending charging.

Applies to 'sensor' type Components that have a charging suspension capability, typically for safety or equipment protection reasons. If Suspension is true, the component can suspend charging when the sensed quantity is outside preset/configured limits.
Temperature(s) of component (in Celsius, by default). Components may have multiple indexed temperature sensors.
Point in time value, in ISO 8601 datetime format. Time zone optional.
Generic timeout value for Component operation (in seconds).
Number of attempts done by a Component.
Single-shot device requires explicit intervention to re-prime/activate to normal.
Vendor or manufacturer of component.
Version date of component in [RFC3339] format.
Version number of hardware
Percentage voltage imbalance in three phase supply.
Points to a communication parent component (data flow source), to allow rendering the communication hierarchy in a UI.
Points to a electrical parent component (energy flow source), to allow rendering the electrical hierarchy in a UI.
Points to a logical parent component, to allow rendering a comprehensive overview of the Charging Station components in a UI.
Points to a physical parent component (container), to allow rendering an overview of the Charging Station component locations in a UI.
Specifies a non-unique label to be used in a hierarchy UI rendering, in place of the unique component name and instance, in case a duplicate label is needed.
If this variable reports a value of true, Clock-Aligned Data is supported.
If this variable reports a value of true, Clock-Aligned Data is enabled
Size (in seconds) of the clock-aligned data interval, intended to be transmitted in the MeterValuesRequest or TransactionEventRequest message.
Clock-aligned measurand(s) to be included in MeterValuesRequest or TransactionEventRequest, every AlignedDataInterval seconds.
If set to true, the Charging Station SHALL NOT send clock aligned meter values when a transaction is ongoing.
If set to true, the Charging Station SHALL include signed meter values in the SampledValueType in theTransactionEventRequest(Ended).
If set to true, the Charging Station SHALL include signed meter values in the SampledValueType in theTransactionEventRequest(Updated).
Size (in seconds) of the clock-aligned data interval, intended to be transmitted in the TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended) message.
Clock-aligned measurands to be included in the meterValues element of TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended), every SampledDataTxEndedInterval seconds from the start of the transaction.
Size (in seconds) of the clock-aligned data interval, intended to be transmitted in the MeterValuesRequest message for location `Upstream` only.
Clock-aligned measurand(s) to be included in MeterValuesReques for location `Upstream` only.
Authorization caching is available, but not necessarily enabled.
If set to true, Authorizaiton caching is enabled.
Indicates how long it takes until a token expires in the authorization cache since it is last used
Cache Entry Replacement Policy: least recently used, least frequently used, first in first out, other custom mechanism.
Indicates the number of bytes currently used by the Authorization Cache. MaxLimit indicates the maximum number of bytes that can be used by the Authorization Cache.
When set to true this variable disables the behavior to request authorization for an idToken that is stored in the cache with a status other than Accepted, as stated in C10.FR.03 and C12.FR.05.
Maximum number of AdditionalInfo items that can be sent in one message.
Whether a remote request to start a transaction in the form of RequestStartTransactionRequest message should be authorized beforehand like a local action to start a transaction.

If set to false, then no authorization is done before starting a transaction or when reading an idToken. If an idToken was provided, then it will be put in the idToken field of the TransactionEventRequest. If no idToken was provided, then idToken in TransactionEventRequest will be left empty and type is set to NoAuthorization.
Whether the Charging Station, when Offline, will start a transaction for locally-authorized identifiers
Whether the Charging Station, when online, will start a transaction for locally-authorized identifiers without waiting for or requesting an AuthorizeResponse from the CSMS.
IdTokens that have this id as groupId belong to the Master Pass Group. Meaning they can stop any ongoing transaction, but cannot start transactions.
Support for unknown offline transactions.
When set to true this instructs the Charging Station to not issue any AuthorizationRequests, but only use Authorization Cache and Local Authorization List to determine validity of idTokens.
The subset of the list of supported IdTokenTypes as defined in Appendix 7. "Standardized values for enumerations as string: IdTokenEnumStringType", that is supported by the Charging Station.
Self-test is active or self-test is started by setting to true.
CHAdeMO protocol number (H'102.0)
Vehicle status (H'102.5.3)
Vehicle is compatible with dynamic control (H'110.0.0)
Vehicle is compatible with high current control (H'110.0.1)
Vehicle is compatible with high voltage control (H'110.1.2)
Auto manufacturer code (H'700.0)
Indicates whether new sessions can be started on EVSEs, while Charging Station is waiting for all EVSEs to become Available in order to start a pending firmware update
This variable reports current availability state for the ChargingStation
Component exists
Charging station model as reported in BootNotification.
Number of alternating current phases connected/available.
Charging station vendor name as reported in BootNotification.
This variable for the ChargingStation component contains a comma-separated list of transaction IDs actively running on the Charging Station.
Contains the current date and time
Date time of the next time offset transition.
This contains the address of the NTP server.
When an NTP client is implemented, this variable can be used to configure the client
If set, then time adjustments with an absolute value in seconds larger than this need to be reported as a security event SettingSystemTime
A Time Offset with respect to Coordinated Universal Time (aka UTC or Greenwich Mean Time) in the form of an [RFC3339] time (zone) offset suffix, including the mandatory + or - prefix.
Via this variable, the Charging Station provides the CSMS with the option to configure multiple clock sources
Configured current local time zone in the format: "Europe/Oslo", "Asia/Singapore" etc. For display purposes.
Information about uri and version that was agreed upon between EV and EVSE in the supportedAppProtocolReq message from ISO 15118. Example: urn:iso:15118:2:2013:MsgDef,2,0
Information from the supportedAppProtocolReq message from ISO 15118. Each priority is given its own variable instance. Example: urn:iso:15118:2:2013:MsgDef,2,0
EVCCID (from ISO 15118 SessionSetupReq)
The PEM encoded X.509 Leaf certificate of the vehicle certificate chain
The PEM encoded X.509 Intermediate certificate of the vehicle certificate chain
The PEM encoded X.509 Intermediate certificate of the vehicle certificate chain
The PEM encoded X.509 Root certificate of the vehicle certificate chain
EV min/max AC current
EV min/max DC current
EV target current from DC_ChargeLoopReq
EV min/max DC voltage
EV target voltage from DC_ChargeLoopReq
EV max power limit
EV max discharge power limit
EV energy capacity / maximum energy request
EV minimum energy request
EV target energy request
EV battery capacity
EV planned departure time
Remaining time to bulk SoC
Remaining time to full SoC
Defined % for bulk SoC
Defined % for full SoC
Current SoC
Charging to bulk SoC is complete
Charging to full SoC is complete
EVTerminationCode to signal error condition
This variable reports current availability state for the Connector. Optional, because already reported in StatusNotification.
Component exists

The Charging Control Protocol applicable to a Connector. CHAdeMO: CHAdeMO protocol, ISO15118: ISO15118 V2G protocol (wired or wireless) as used with CCS, CPPWM: IEC61851-1 / SAE J1772  protocol (ELV DC & PWM signalling via Control Pilot wire), Uncontrolled: No charging power management applies (e.g. Schuko socket), Undetermined: Yet to be determined (e.g. before plugged in), Unknown: Not determinable, NOTE: ChargeProtocol is distinct from and orthogonal to connectorType.

A value of ConnectorEnumType (See part 2) plus additionally: cGBT, cChaoJi, OppCharge. Specific type of connector, including sub-variant information. Note: Distinct and orthogonal to Charging Protocol, Power Type, Phases.
Number of alternating current phases connected/available.
Battery slot status of swapping station: Open, Locked, Faulted.
IEC 61851-1 states ("A" to "E")
Custom implementation <vendorId> has been enabled.
This variable defines the names of custom triggers that Charging Station supports in a customTrigger field of TriggerMessageRequest.
Maximum number of bytes in a message related to instance name: GetReport, GetVariables, SetVariables
Maximum number of bytes in a message related to instance name: GetReport, GetVariables, SetVariables
Maximum number of bytes in a message related to instance name: GetReport, GetVariables, SetVariables
The limit to the following fields: SetVariableData.attributeValue and VariableCharacteristics.valueList. The max size of these values will always remain equal.
Maximum number of ComponentVariable entries in message related to the instance name: GetReport, GetVariables, SetVariables
Maximum number of ComponentVariable entries in message related to the instance name: GetReport, GetVariables, SetVariables
Maximum number of ComponentVariable entries in message related to the instance name: GetReport, GetVariables, SetVariables
The limit to the following fields: GetVariableResult.attributeValue, VariableAttribute.value and EventData.actualValue. The max size of these values will always remain equal.
Can be used to limit the following fields: SetVariableData.attributeValue, GetVariableResult.attributeValue, VariableAttribute.value, VariableCharacteristics.valueList and EventData.actualValue.
Whether display messages are supported.
Amount of different messages that are currently configured in this Charging Station, via SetDisplayMessageRequest
Whether display messages are enabled.
List of the states during which to display a message supported by this Charging Station.
List of message formats supported by this Charging Station.
List of the priorities supported by this Charging Station.
Default language code, per RFC 5646, of this Charging Station. Languages that are supported are listed in the valuesList.
Can be used to announce that an EVSE can be reset individually
This variable reports current availability state for the EVSE
Component exists
The name of the EVSE in the string format as required by ISO 15118 and IEC 63119-2.
The variableCharacteristic maxLimit, that holds the maximum power that this EVSE can provide, is required. The Actual value of the instantaneous (real) power is desired, but not required.

The variableCharacteristic maxLimit holds the maximum rated discharge power that this EVSE can provide.  The variableCharacteristic maxSet holds the maximum configured discharge power that this EVSE can provide. The Actual value of the instantaneous (real) discharge power is recommended to be supported, but not required. Discharge power is represented by a positive value.
Number of alternating current phases connected/available.
When DCInputPhaseControl is true, then the values of numberPhases and PhaseToUse in a ChargingSchedulePeriodType will select the input phases from the grid to be used by the DC EVSE.
The name of the EVSE in the string format as required by ISO 15118 and IEC 63119-2. Example: "DE*ICE*E*1234567890*1"
This variable reports the current transaction charging state for an EVSE.
This variable contains the transaction ID of the running transaction at the EVSE. If no transaction is active, it is an empty string.
Total energy transferred: e.g.  from EV during (ongoing or terminated) charging session (in wH by default)
Cumulative export kWh register value, such as from a (certified) fiscal energy meter.
Total energy transferred.
Cumulative export kWh register value, such as from a (certified) fiscal energy meter.
The public key for a meter connected to a specific EVSE.
If this variable exists and has the value true, then Charging Station can provide a contract certificate that it cannot validate, to the CSMS for validation as part of the AuthorizeRequest.
If this variable is true, then Charging Station will try to validate a contract certificate when it is offline
The ID of the SECC in string format as defined by ISO15118.
Maximum number of allowed schedule periods.
The requested energy transfer mode.
If true, then Charging Station shall request a metering receipt from EV.
The countryName of the SECC in the ISO 3166-1 format. It is used as the countryName (C) of the SECC leaf certificate. Example: "DE"

The organizationName of the CSO operating the charging station. It is used as the organizationName (O) of the SECC leaf certificate. Example: "John Doe Charging Services Ltd" Note: This value will usually be identical to SecurityCtrlr.OrganizationName, but it does not have to be.

If this variable is true, then ISO 15118 plug and charge as described by use case C07 - Authorization using Contract Certificates is enabled. If this variable is false, then ISO 15118 plug and charge as described by use case C07 - Authorization using Contract Certificates is disabled.

If this variable is true, then ISO 15118 V2G Charging Station certificate installation as described by use case A02 - Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS and A03 - Update Charging Station Certificate initiated by the Charging Station is enabled.  If this variable is false, then ISO 15118 V2G Charging Station certificate installation as described by use case A02 - Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS and A03 - Update Charging Station Certificate initiated by the Charging Station is disabled.

If this variable is true, then ISO 15118 contract certificate installation/update as described by use case M01 - Certificate installation EV and M02 - Certificate Update EV is enabled. If this variable is false, then ISO 15118 contract certificate installation/update as described by use case M01 - Certificate installation EV and M02 - Certificate Update EV is disabled.
When present, this variable tells CSMS whether Charging Station uses OCSP or CRL to check for revoked certificates.
The SECC (EVSE) uses the NotificationMaxDelay element in the EVSEStatus to indicate the time in seconds until it expects the EVCC (EV) to react on the action request indicated in EVSENotification.
If set to 'True' the SECC (EVSE) is capable of ServiceRenegotiation.
A comma-separated list of all providers (eMSPs) that are supported on this Charging Station. The providers are listed using country and provider ID from the EMAID, as defined in ISO 15118-20.
The maximum number of priceRuleStacks and priceLevelScheduleEntries that Charging Station is able to accept in a ChargingScheduleType.
A string with the following comma-separated items:  <uri>,<major>,<minor>. <uri> is in the format as used in the SupportedAppProtocolReq message from ISO 15118-2 and ISO 15118-20.
Local Authorization List is available.
Maximum number of bytes in a SendLocalList message.
If this variable exists and reports a value of true, Local Authorization List is enabled.
Amount of IdTokens currently in the Local Authorization List
Maximum number of records in SendLocalList

variables.name: ACCurrent
variables.description: RMS AC Current (in amperes). For 3-phase circuits, each phase (and optional neutral) is represented by a Variable instance equal to a value of the PhaseEnumType (e.g. L1,N). Unkeyed values reported for a Component declared to be multi-phase are assumed to be an average of all per-phase readings and written values are common per-phase settings. Example(s): ChargingStation: Total AC current consumption (all EVSE’s, ancillaries), EVSE: Total current consumed by EVSE: includes losses (AC->DC) and EVSE specific ancillaries (e.g. fans), ElectricalFeed: Inflow AC current on feed

variables.name: ACVoltage
variables.description: RMS AC Voltage (in volts). For 3-phase circuits, each phase (and optional neutral) is represented by a Variable instance equal to a value of the PhaseEnumType (e.g. L1,N). Unkeyed values reported for a Component declared to be multi-phase are assumed to be an average ofall per-phase readings and written values are common per-phase settings. Example(s): ElectricalFeed: Input Voltage

variables.name: Active
variables.description: Component is in its non-resting / active state: e.g: On, Engaged, Locked. Some Components may have secondary functions that have corresponding Active Variables with an explicit Variable instance., Note: Monitoring of changes in the Active state of any Component can be specified by setting Delta monitoring on the boolean value with a delta values of 1. Setting/clearing an Active Variable activates/stops the associated functionality, where remotely controllable. Only components that are Available and Enabled can be in the Active state.

variables.description: If set to true the Charging Station supports tariffs with conditions.

variables.name: ConfigurationValueSize
variables.description: This Configuration Variable can be used to limit the following fields: SetVariableData.attributeValue and VariableCharacteristics.valuesList.

variables.name: ConnectedTime
variables.description: Time since logical connection established

variables.name: ConnectorType
variables.description: A value of ConnectorStringEnumType (See Appendix 7). Specific type of connector, including sub-variant information. Note: Distinct and orthogonal to Charging Protocol, Power Type, Phases.

variables.name: ContractCertificateInstallationEnabled
variables.description: If this variable is _true_, then ISO 15118 contract certificate installation/update as described by use case M01 - Certificate installation EV

variables.name: ContractValidationOffline
variables.description: If this variable is _true_, then Charging Station will try to validate a contract certificate when it is offline.

variables.name: Count
variables.description: General purpose integer count variable for Component state reporting

variables.name: CountryName
variables.description: The countryName of the SECC in the ISO 3166-1 format.

variables.name: Currency
variables.description: Currency in a ISO 4217 formatted currency code.

variables.name: CurrentImbalance
variables.description: Percentage current imbalance in an AC three phase supply.

variables.name: CustomImplementationEnabled
variables.description: This standard configuration variable is used to enable/disable the custom implementation named in the *variableInstance*.

variables.name: CustomTriggers
variables.description: This variable defines the names of custom triggers that Charging Station supports in a _customTrigger_ field of TriggerMessageRequest.

variables.name: DCCurrent
variables.description: DC Current (in amperes). May be an instantaneous measurement, or a period average, depending on context/equipment.

variables.name: DCInputPhaseControl
variables.description: When DCInputPhaseControl is true, then the values of _numberPhases_ and _PhaseToUse_ in a ChargingSchedulePeriodType will select the input phases from the grid to be used by the DC EVSE.

variables.name: DCVoltage
variables.description: DC Voltage (volts). May be an instantaneous measurement, or a period average, depending on context/equipment.

variables.name: DataText
variables.description: Text associated with a Component, e.g. a Display.

variables.name: DateTime
variables.description: Point in time value, in [RFC3339] datetime format. Time zone optional.

variables.name: DepartureTime
variables.description: Time in [RFC3339] datetime format, when an EV intends to leave the charging station.

variables.name: DisablePostAuthorize
variables.description: When set to _true_ this variable disables the behavior to request authorization for an

variables.name: DisableRemoteAuthorization
variables.description: When set to _true_ this instructs the Charging Station to not issue any AuthorizationRequests, but only use Authorization Cache and Local Authorization List to determine validity of idTokens.

variables.name: DisplayMessages
variables.description: Maximum number of different messages that can configured in this Charging Station simultaneous, via SetDisplayMessageRequest.

variables.name: ECVariant
variables.description: Production series variants reflecting internal design changes or sub-component substitutions not affecting external functionality.

variables.name: EVConnectionTimeOut
variables.description: Interval from between 'starting' of a transaction until incipient transaction is automatically canceled, due to failure of EV driver to (correctly) insert the charging cable connector(s) into the appropriate socket(s).

variables.name: ElectricalParent
variables.description: Points to a electrical parent component (energy flow source), to allow rendering the electrical hierarchy in a UI.

variables.name: Enabled
variables.description: The Component is Enabled for operation. For Available components that cannot be selectively (remotely) enabled / disabled, this value is always true. Note: Available cannot be false of Enabled is true, so during inventory reporting, Enabled=1 also logically states Available=true

variables.name: Energy
variables.description: Energy quantity (in Wh) for reporting/configuring values related to stored energy (i.e. not transferred energy).

variables.name: EnergyCapacity
variables.description: Energy capacity in Wh of an energy storage device.

variables.name: EnergyExport
variables.description: Total energy transferred: e.g.  from EV during (ongoing or terminated) charging session (in wH by default)

variables.name: EnergyExportRegister
variables.description: Cumulative export kWh register value, such as from a (certified) fiscal energy meter.

variables.name: EnergyImport
variables.description: Total energy transferred.

variables.name: EnergyImportRegister
variables.description: Cumulative export kWh register value, such as from a (certified) fiscal energy meter.

variables.name: Entries
variables.description: General purpose variable for reporting/managing numbers of entries in repetitive data structures. maxLimit characteristic reports maximum possible entries.

variables.name: ExternalConfigChangeDate
variables.description: Date/time when the configuration was changed externally, i.e. outside of CSMS, for example by a local service action.

variables.name: ExternalConstraintsProfileDisallowed
variables.description: Indicates whether a Charging Station allows an external system to submit a `ChargingStationExternalConstraints` charging profile.

variables.name: ExternalControlSignalsEnabled
variables.description: Indicates whether a Charging Station is able to respond to external control signals that influence charging. If the variable is true, but CSMS has set <<configkey-external-constraints-profile-disallowed>> = true, then external control signals are only allowed during a charging profile with a _chargingProfilePeriod_ = `ExternalLimits` or `ExternalSetpoint`.

variables.name: Fallback
variables.description: Component is operating in a fallback, or backup mode. In inventory reports, a Value of 1 for the maxLimit characteristic indicates that the component can enter a fallback state (i.e. a fallback mode is present).

variables.name: FanSpeed
variables.description: Fan Speed (in RPM). A value of 0 represents stopped/stalled. An empty value indicates that fan speed cannot be read.

variables.name: FieldLength
variables.description: This variable is used to report the length of <field> in <message> when it is larger

variables.name: FileTransferProtocols
variables.description: List of supported file transfer protocols.

variables.name: FirmwareVersion
variables.description: Version number of firmware.

variables.name: Force
variables.description: Reports (impact) force/ acceleration values (estimates) in one or more directions, in units of Newtons or “g”. Multiple force readings in different (orthogonal) dimensions may be reported using Variable instance values, such as Down, Right, Forward.

variables.name: Formats
variables.description: List of message formats supported by this Charging Station. Possible values: ASCII, HTML, URI, UTF-8.

variables.name: Frequency
variables.description: Frequency of AC power, signal, or component operation.

variables.name: FrequencySchedule
variables.description: A JSON-formatted string with an array of { _time, freq_ } pairs, in which _time_ is

variables.name: FuseRating
variables.description: Current rating of a fuse/breaker. Variable instances keyed by phase identifier (L1/L2/L3/N).

variables.name: HeartbeatInterval
variables.description: Interval of inactivity (no OCPP exchanges) with CSMS after which the Charging Station should send HeartbeatRequest.

variables.name: Height
variables.description: Height above(+)/below(-) reference level (ground level unless context demands otherwise).

variables.name: Humidity
variables.description: The relative humidity in %.

variables.name: Hysteresis
variables.description: Specifies the width of a 'dead band' (as a percentage of the threshold) around the central value of a threshold setting (e.g. MinSet, MaxSet, monitor thresholds) to avoid repeated triggering when the measured quantity lies close to the threshold and is subject to small variations.

variables.name: ICCID
variables.description: ICCID (Integrated Circuit Card IDentifier) of mobile data SIM card.

variables.name: IMSI
variables.description: IMSI (International Mobile Subscriber Identity) number of mobile data SIM card

variables.name: ISO15118EvseId
variables.description: EVSE ID in string format as used in ISO 15118 and IEC 63119-2

variables.name: IdToken
variables.description: The IdToken used to authorize a charging transaction.

variables.name: Identity
variables.description: The Charging Station identity.

variables.name: Impedance
variables.description: Impedance: Primary value is real (resistive only) impedance. Where a complex impedance is to be reported, the imaginary part (reactance) must be represented with a separate Variable instance value of 'reactance'. Reactance values are expressed at the (nominal) relevant operating frequency of the Component (e.g. 50/60Hz for mains electricity feed).

variables.name: Interval
variables.description: Minimum Interval (in seconds) between (attempted) operations.

variables.name: ItemsPerMessage
variables.description: Maximum number of ComponentVariable entries that can be sent in one GetReportRequest or GetMonitoringReportRequest message.

variables.name: Label
variables.description: Label for a component. Specifies a non-unique label to be used in a hierarchy UI rendering, in place of the unique component name and instance

variables.name: Language
variables.description: Default language code, per RFC 5646, of this Charging Station.

variables.name: Length
variables.description: General Purpose linear distance measure.

variables.name: LifeTime
variables.description: Indicates how long it takes until a token expires in the authorization cache since it is last used.

variables.name: Light
variables.description: (Ambient) light level. The value is in Lux.

variables.name: LimitChangeSignificance
variables.description: If at the Charging Station side a change in the limit in a ChargingProfile is lower than this percentage, the Charging Station MAY skip sending a NotifyChargingLimitRequest or a TransactionEventRequest message to the CSMS.

variables.name: LocalAuthorizeOffline
variables.description: Whether the Charging Station, when _Offline_, will start a transaction for locally-authorized identifiers.

variables.name: LocalLoadBalancing
variables.description: Variable with instances to control local load-balancing.

variables.name: LocalPreAuthorize
variables.description: Whether the Charging Station, when online, will start a transaction for locally-authorized identifiers without waiting for or requesting an AuthorizeResponse from the CSMS.

variables.name: LogicalParent
variables.description: Points to a logical parent component, to allow rendering a comprehensive overview of the Charging Station components in a UI.

variables.name: Manufacturer
variables.description: Component Manufacturer name

variables.name: MasterPassGroupId
variables.description: IdTokens that have this id as groupId belong to the Master Pass Group.

variables.name: MaxCertificateChainSize
variables.description: This configuration variable can be used to limit the size of the 'certificateChain' field from the CertificateSignedRequest PDU.

variables.name: MaxEnergyOnInvalidId
variables.description: Maximum amount of energy in Wh delivered when an identifier is deauthorized by the CSMS after start of a transaction.

variables.name: MaxExternalConstraintsId
variables.description: Defines the highest value that a charging profile id of a `ChargingStationExternalConstraints` profile in the Charging Station can have.

variables.name: MaxPriceElements
variables.description: For ISO15118Ctrlr: The maximum number of _priceRuleStacks_ and _priceLevelScheduleEntries_ that Charging Station is able to accept in a ChargingScheduleType.

variables.name: Measurands
variables.description: Measurand(s) to be included in <<metervaluesrequest,MeterValuesRequest>> or <<transactioneventrequest,TransactionEventRequest>>

variables.name: Message
variables.description: Specific stored message for display.

variables.name: MessageAttemptInterval
variables.description: How long the Charging Station should wait before resubmitting a TransactionEventRequest message that the CSMS failed to process.

variables.name: MessageAttempts
variables.description: How often the Charging Station should try to submit a TransactionEventRequest message when the CSMS fails to process it.

variables.name: MessageTimeout
variables.description: The purpose of the message timeout is to be able to consider a request message as not sent and continue with other tasks when the message did not arrive due to communication errors or software failure.

variables.name: MinimumStatusDuration
variables.description: Minimum duration that a Charging Station or EVSE status is stable before StatusNotificationRequest is sent to the CSMS.

variables.name: Mode
variables.description: Operating mode string from among valid options (communicated by OptionList, etc. during capability/configuration  discovery).

variables.name: Model
variables.description: Manufacturer's Model code/number of Component, including suffixes etc. to identify functional, regional or linguistic variation, but NOT engineering change level internal 				variation not affecting external behaviour, etc.

variables.name: NetworkAddress
variables.description: Current network address of a Component.

variables.name: NetworkConfigurationPriority
variables.description: A comma separated ordered list of the priority of the possible Network Connection Profiles. The list of possible available profile slots for the network configuration profiles SHALL be reported, via the valuesList characteristic of this Variable.

variables.name: NetworkProfileConnectionAttempts
variables.description: Specifies the number of connection attempts the Charging Station executes before switching to a different profile.

variables.name: NextTimeOffsetTransitionDateTime
variables.description: Date time of the next time offset transition. On this date time, the clock displayed to the EV driver will be given the new offset as configured via `TimeOffsetNextTransition`.

variables.name: NonEvseSpecific
variables.description: For ReservationCtrlr: If this configuration variable is present and set to _true_: Charging Station supports reservation where EVSE id is not specified.

variables.name: NotificationMaxDelay
variables.description: For ISO15118Ctrlr: The SECC (EVSE) uses the NotificationMaxDelay element in the EVSEStatus to indicate the time in seconds until it expects the EVCC (EV) to react on the action request indicated in EVSENotification.

variables.name: NotifyChargingLimitWithSchedules
variables.description: Indicates if the Charging Station should include the externally set charging limit/schedule in the message when it sends a NotifyChargingLimitRequest message.

variables.name: NtpServerUri
variables.description: This contains the address of the NTP server.

variables.name: NtpSource
variables.description: Use the NTP server provided via DHCP, or use the manually configured NTP server.

variables.name: OfflineQueuingSeverity
variables.description: When set and the Charging Station is _offline_, the Charging Station shall queue any NotifyEventRequest messages triggered by a monitor with a severity number equal to or lower than the severity configured here.

variables.name: OfflineTariffFallbackMessage
variables.description: Message (and/or tariff information) to be shown to an EV Driver when Charging Station is offline.

variables.name: OfflineThreshold
variables.description: When the offline period of a Charging Station exceeds the `OfflineThreshold` it is recommended to send a StatusNotificationRequest for all its Connectors when the Charging Station is back online.

variables.name: OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled
variables.description: If this key exists and is true, the Charging Station supports Unknown Offline Authorization.

variables.name: Operated
variables.description: The Component operated in an instantaneous, transient, or immediately self-resetting pattern. Used only in event notifications, where it is always true.

variables.name: OperatingTimes
variables.description: Recurring operating times in iCalendar RRULE format.

variables.name: OrganizationName
variables.description: The organizationName of the CSO operating the charging station.

variables.name: Overload
variables.description: Component is in Overload state.

variables.name: Percent
variables.description: Generic dimensionless value reporting/setting value.

variables.name: PeriodsPerSchedule
variables.description: Maximum number of periods that may be defined per ChargingSchedule.

variables.name: PhaseRotation
variables.description: This variable describes the phase rotation of a Component relative to its parent Component, using a

variables.name: Phases3to1
variables.description: If defined and true, this Charging Station supports switching from 3 to 1 phase during a transaction.

variables.name: PhysicalParent
variables.description: Points to a physical parent component (container), to allow rendering an overview of the Charging Station component locations in a UI.

variables.name: PnCEnabled
variables.description: If this variable is _true_, then ISO 15118 plug and charge as described by use case C07 - Authorization using Contract Certificates is enabled.

variables.name: Policy
variables.description: Cache Entry Replacement Policy: least recently used, least frequently used, first in first out, other custom mechanism.

variables.name: PostChargingTime
variables.description: Elapsed time in seconds since last substantive energy transfer

variables.name: Power
variables.description: Instantaneous (real) Power (measured/calculated, including power factor for AC). Where a component (e.g. AC to DC Power Converter) has multiple power measurements, the default (unkeyed) instance is “input” power.

variables.name: Present
variables.description: Component exists, but might not be locally configured/wired for use, nor (remotely) Enabled.

variables.name: Problem
variables.description: Component itself has a 'Problem' condition that impacts in any significant way on its normal operation. By definition, 'Problem' state includes (logical OR) 'Fault' state. 'Problem' specifically INCLUDES inability to operate that is propagated (up/down/sideways) from any other associated/connected/containing/contained Component.

variables.name: ProfileStackLevel
variables.description: Maximum acceptable value for _stackLevel_ in a ChargingProfile.

variables.name: Protecting
variables.description: Applies to 'sensor' type Components that have an associated protection capability, whereby they can  disconnect power (e.g. using the main PowerContactor) if the sensed quantity is outside preset/configured limits. If Protecting is true, the Component is actively preventing/interrupting charging.

variables.name: ProtocolAgreed
variables.description: For ConnectedEV: A string with the following comma-separated items: “<uri>,<major>,<minor>”. This is the protocol uri and version information that was agreed upon between EV and EVSE in the supportedAppProtocolReq handshake from ISO 15118.

variables.name: ProtocolSupported
variables.description: For ISO15118Ctrlr: A string with the following comma-separated items: “<uri>,<major>,<minor>”. <uri> is in the format as used in the SupportedAppProtocolReq message from ISO 15118-2 and ISO 15118-20. This variable has at most 20 instances, one for each supported protocol version.

variables.name: ProtocolSupportedByEV
variables.description: For ConnectedEV: A string with the following comma-separated items: “<uri>,<major>,<minor>”. This is information from the SupportedAppProtocolReq message from ISO 15118. Each priority is given its own variable instance. Priority is a number from 1 to 20 as a string.

variables.name: PublicKey
variables.description: Configuration variable that can be used to retrieve the public key for a meter connected to a specific EVSE.

variables.name: PublicKeyWithSignedMeterValue
variables.description: This Configuration Variable can be used to configure whether a public key needs to be sent with a signed meter value.

variables.name: QueueAllMessages
variables.description: When this variable is set to _true_, the Charging Station will queue all message until they are delivered to the CSMS.

variables.name: RateUnit
variables.description: A list of supported quantities (A, W) for use in a ChargingSchedule.

variables.name: RegisterValuesWithoutPhases
variables.description: If this variable reports a value of _true_, then meter values of measurand `Energy.Active.Import.Register` will only report the total energy over all phases without reporting the individual phase values.

variables.name: RemainingTimeBulk
variables.description: Number of seconds remaining to charge to bulk state of charge, given by StateOfChargeBulk.

variables.name: RemainingTimeFull
variables.description: Number of seconds remaining to charge to 100% state of charge.

variables.name: ReportingValueSize
variables.description: This Configuration Variable can be used to limit the following fields: GetVariableResult.attributeValue, VariableAttribute.value and EventData.actualValue.

variables.name: RequestMeteringReceipt
variables.description: For ISO15118Ctrlr: If this variable is _true_, then Charging Station shall request a metering receipt

variables.name: ResetRetries
variables.description: Number of times to retry a reset of the Charging Station when a reset was unsuccessful.

variables.name: ResumptionTimeout
variables.description: This variable defines the maximum number of seconds that a transaction may be interrupted by a power outage and still be resumed afterwards.

variables.name: SampledMeasurands
variables.description: The set of measurands to be sampled by the DataCollector component.

variables.name: SamplingInterval
variables.description: The sampling interval in *seconds*.

variables.name: SeccId
variables.description: The name of the SECC in the string format as required by ISO 15118.

variables.name: SecurityProfile
variables.description: This configuration variable is used to report the security profile used by the Charging Station.

variables.name: SendDuringIdle
variables.description: For AlignedDataCtrlr: If set to _true_, the Charging Station SHALL only send clock aligned meter values when there is no transaction ongoing.

variables.name: SerialNumber
variables.description: Serial number of Component.

variables.name: ServiceRenegotiationSupport
variables.description: For ISO15118Ctrlr: If set to 'True' the SECC (EVSE) is capable of ServiceRenegotiation.

variables.name: SignReadings
variables.description: If set to _true_, the Charging Station SHALL include signed meter values in the TransactionEventRequest(Ended).

variables.name: SignStartedReadings
variables.description: If set to _true_, the Charging Station SHALL include signed meter values for _context_ = `Transaction.Begin` in the _metervalues_ field in the TransactionEventRequest(Started or Updated).

variables.name: SignUpdatedReadings
variables.description: If set to _true_, the Charging Station SHALL include signed meter values in the _metervalues_ field in the TransactionEventRequest(Updated).

variables.name: SignalStrength
variables.description: (Radio/Wired/Optical) data signal strength, in ASU (typically 0-31 or 99 for unknown). Or dbmW (typically -140 to -50).

variables.name: SlotStatus
variables.description: This variable represents the status of the door of the battery slot.

variables.name: SoC
variables.description: SoC of the component BatteryCartridge which refers to the battery that is inserted at the EVSE.

variables.name: SoH
variables.description: SoH of the component BatteryCartridge which refers to the battery that is inserted at the EVSE.

variables.name: State
variables.description: A state code or name identifier string, to allow the internal state of  components to be reported and/or  controlled

variables.name: StateOfCharge
variables.description: Energy Storage Device (e.g. battery) state of charge, expressed as a percentage of nominal design 0-100% operating range. The value of StateOfCharge.maxSet represents the maximum state of charge for a full battery and is usually at or near 100%.

variables.name: StateOfChargeBulk
variables.description: Energy Storage Device (e.g. battery) state of charge up to which fast charging is possible. Above this percentage charging speed will drop significantly.

variables.name: StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect
variables.description: When set to _true_, the Charging Station SHALL deauthorize the transaction when the cable is unplugged from the EV.

variables.name: StopTxOnInvalidId
variables.description: Whether the Charging Station will deauthorize an ongoing transaction when it receives a non- _Accepted_ authorization status in TransactionEventResponse for this transaction.

variables.name: Storage
variables.description: In bytes. Amount of storage occupied. Storage(maxLimit) specifies absolute limit Storage(MaxSet) restricts usage to specified Max, if supported.

variables.name: SupplyPhases
variables.description: Number of alternating current phases connected/available. 1 or 3 for AC, 0 means DC (no alternating phases). Null value indicates that the number of phases (e.g. in use) is unknown.

variables.name: SupportsExpiryDateTime
variables.description: For LocalAuthListCtrlr: When set to _true_ Charging Station will disregard idTokens for authorization as if not present in the Local Authorization List when current date/time is past the value of _cacheExpiryDateTime_.

variables.name: Suspending
variables.description: If Suspending is true, the Component can is currently suspending charging.

variables.name: Suspension
variables.description: Applies to 'sensor' type Components that have a charging suspension capability, typically for safety or equipment protection reasons. If Suspension is true, the component can suspend charging when the sensed quantity is outside preset/configured limits.

variables.name: TariffFallbackMessage
variables.description: Message (and/or tariff information) to be shown to an EV Driver when there is no driver specific tariff information available.

variables.name: Temperature
variables.description: Temperature(s) of component (in Celsius, by default). Components may have multiple indexed temperature sensors.

variables.name: Time
variables.description: Point in time value, in ISO 8601 datetime format. Time zone optional.

variables.name: TimeAdjustmentReportingThreshold
variables.description: When the clock time is adjusted forwards or backwards for more then TimeAdjustmentReportingThreshold number of seconds, a SecurityEventNotification( 'SettingSystemTime' ) is sent by the charging station.

variables.name: TimeOffset
variables.description: A Time Offset with respect to Coordinated Universal Time (aka UTC or Greenwich Mean Time) in the form of an [RFC3339] time (zone) offset suffix, including the mandatory “+” or “-“ prefix.

variables.name: TimeSource
variables.description: Via this variable, the Charging Station provides the CSMS with the option to configure a clock source.

variables.name: TimeZone
variables.description: Configured current local time zone in the format: 'Europe/Oslo', 'Asia/Singapore' etc.

variables.name: Timeout
variables.description: Generic timeout value for Component operation (in seconds).

variables.name: Timeout
variables.description: For BatterySwapCtrlr: Timeout in seconds in which a set of batteries must be inserted or removed after successful authorization.

variables.name: Token
variables.description: String of bytes representing an ID token.

variables.name: TokenType
variables.description: Type of Token. Value is one of IdTokenEnumStringType (See Appendix 7).

variables.name: TotalCostFallbackMessage
variables.description: Message to be shown to an EV Driver when the Charging Station cannot retrieve the cost for a transaction at the end of the transaction.

variables.name: Tries
variables.description: Number of attempts done by a Component.

variables.name: Tripped
variables.description: Single-shot device requires explicit intervention to re-prime/activate to normal.

variables.name: TxBeforeAcceptedEnabled
variables.description: With this configuration variable the Charging Station can be configured to allow charging before having received a BootNotificationResponse with status: Accepted.

variables.name: TxEndedInterval
variables.description: Interval between sampling of metering (or other) data, intended to be transmitted in the TransactionEventRequest(Ended) message.

variables.name: TxEndedMeasurands
variables.description: Sampled measurands to be included in the _meterValues_ element of TransactionEventRequest(Ended).

variables.name: TxStartPoint
variables.description: Start points for a transaction.

variables.name: TxStartedMeasurands
variables.description: Sampled measurands to be included in the _meterValues_ element of TransactionEventRequest(Started).

variables.name: TxStopPoint
variables.description: Stop points of a transaction.

variables.name: TxUpdatedInterval
variables.description: Interval between sampling of metering (or other) data, intended to be transmitted in the TransactionEventRequest(Updated) message.

variables.name: TxUpdatedMeasurands
variables.description: Sampled measurands to be included in the _meterValues_ element of TransactionEventRequest(Updated).

variables.name: UnlockOnEVSideDisconnect
variables.description: When set to true, the Charging Station SHALL unlock the cable on the Charging Station side when the cable is unplugged at the EV.

variables.name: V2GCertificateInstallationEnabled
variables.description: If this variable is _true_, then ISO 15118 V2G Charging Station certificate installation as described by use case A02 - Update Charging Station Certificate by request of CSMS

variables.name: VehicleCertificate
variables.description: For ConnectedEV: The PEM encoded X.509 leaf/intermediate/root certificate when present in the vehicle certificate chain.

variables.name: VehicleId
variables.description: ID that EV provides to charging station. Encoded as a hexbinary string. In ISO 15118 the EVCCID is 6 bytes (MAC address), in CHAdeMO the vehicle id can be 24 bytes.

variables.name: VersionNumber
variables.description: Version number of hardware

variables.name: VoltageImbalance
variables.description: Percentage voltage imbalance in three phase supply.
